questions.question: The function f(x) = 3sin(2x) has amplitude A and period P. What is A + P?
questions.explanation: For f(x) = a·sin(bx), amplitude A = |a| = 3 and period P = 2π/b = 2π/2 = π. Therefore A + P = 3 + π.

questions.question: A triangle has sides a = 5, b = 7, and angle C = 60°. Find the length of side c using the law of cosines. Round to one decimal place.
If sin(θ) = 3/5 and θ is in the second quadrant, what is cos(θ)?
questions.explanation: Use sin²(θ) + cos²(θ) = 1: (3/5)² + cos²(θ) = 1, so cos²(θ) = 16/25. Since θ is in quadrant II, cos(θ) is negative: cos(θ) = −4/5.

questions.question: A triangle has angles A = 50°, B = 70°, and side a = 10. Find side b using the law of sines. Round to one decimal place.
The graph of y = −cos(x) + 2 is shifted right by π/2 units. Write the equation of the resulting function.
questions.explanation: Shifting right by π/2 replaces x with (x − π/2): y = −cos(x − π/2) + 2. Since cos(x − π/2) = sin(x), this simplifies to y = −sin(x) + 2.

questions.question: In a triangle, a = 8, b = 6, and c = 7. Find cos(B) as a simplified fraction.
questions.explanation: Law of cosines: b² = a² + c² − 2ac·cos(B), so 36 = 64 + 49 − 2(8)(7)cos(B) = 113 − 112cos(B). Thus 112cos(B) = 77, giving cos(B) = 77/112 = 11/16.
